description: Attrition and noise metrics for the skills_uri fabrication on apps/api/fixtures/cv/cv_fixture_v0.txt.

attrition_ratios.coverage_of_explicit_cv_skills_approx: 9 / 26 = 34.6%  (direct hits on declared tool names: Python, SQL, MySQL, PostgreSQL, NoSQL, DevOps, ETL, Excel->tableur, Agile)

fuzzy_matching_contribution.note: map_skill called with enable_fuzzy=False from both baseline_parser.run_baseline_from_tokens and skill_filter.strict_filter_skills. Fuzzy fallback never executes in this pipeline.

layer_1_SKILL_ALIASES_in_esco_extract_py.role: expands tokens in the set BEFORE noise filter and map_skill
examples_captured_in_raw_tokens: excel -> utiliser un logiciel de tableur
agile -> gestion de projets par méthode agile + développement par itérations
data -> analyse de données + exploration de données
examples_silently_not_expanded: dashboards -> logiciel de visualisation des données (expected, absent)
machine learning -> apprentissage automatique (bigram itself not captured)

layer_2_esco_alias_fr_v0_jsonl_in_skill_.role: direct URI lookup BEFORE map_skill, for FR CV vocabulary
examples_captured: reporting -> analyse de données (dedup with existing URI)

critical_code_observations.uri_collapse_not_on_production_path: apps/api/src/profile/baseline_parser.py:86 assigns profile.skills_uri from strict_filter_skills.validated_items. The collapse_to_uris call at lines 92-121 runs as a debug shadow and only populates skills_uri_collapsed_dupes / skills_dupes. collapse_to_uris is OFF the production scoring path.
critical_code_observations.whitelist_regex_bug: apps/api/src/esco/extract.py:272: re.search(rf'\\b{re.escape(skill)}\\b', normalized). The rf'...' raw f-string yields '\\b' (literal backslash-b) not '\b' (word boundary). WHITELIST_SKILLS capture is effectively dead code. Tokens in WHITELIST_SKILLS still appear because they are captured by the standard per-word loop.
critical_code_observations.double_expansion_injection: SKILL_ALIASES entries with multi-value lists (e.g., agile -> [2 ESCO labels]) inject BOTH as separate raw tokens, each producing its own ESCO URI — no selection heuristic.
critical_code_observations.no_context_gate: Alias layers operate on token equality only. No context window, no per-occurrence attribution ('sales TEAMS' vs 'sales SKILL') — see 'sales' -> 'argumentaire de vente' false positive.
critical_code_observations.no_geographic_guard: ESCO lookup admits 'paris' (city -> ESCO concept for 'wager'). No stoplist for proper nouns / city names, despite extract.py treating 'STOPWORDS' as the only filter.
